body: {"ac_map":[{"parent_ac":"Implement the first bounded slice of the parent bead.","coverage":"Collectively covered: A.1 delivers runner skeleton + matrix expansion; A.2 delivers shell harness-adapters; A.3 delivers harbor-runner Docker image + task-executor wrapper; A.4 delivers per-cell loop, flock concurrency, signal handling, and preflight subcommand. Together these constitute the bash runner + shell adapters + harbor-runner image + preflight scope named in the parent title."},{"parent_ac":"cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./... passes.","coverage":"A.1 AC4, A.2 AC4, A.3 AC4, A.4 AC7."},{"parent_ac":"lefthook run pre-commit passes.","coverage":"A.1 AC5, A.2 AC5, A.3 AC5, A.4 AC8."}],"child_ids":["fizeau-3e41af01","fizeau-e74ec147","fizeau-ad47f7c8","fizeau-b49d5ea7"],"rationale":"Split along the four natural seams in the parent scope: (1) outer runner shell — arg parsing, YAML loading, matrix expansion — which can be built and unit-tested in isolation against fixtures; (2) shell port of harness adapters, which has a clean input/output contract (profile.json → command-spec.json) testable for parity against the Python originals without needing the new runner wired up; (3) harbor-runner Docker image + task-executor wrapper, which is the riskiest piece (eliminates host Python) and benefits from landing independently so the image build and adapter packaging can be reviewed on their own; (4) the execution engine — per-cell loop, flock concurrency, signal handling, preflight — which composes the prior three and is where the subtle correctness work lives. Ordering A.1→A.2/A.3 (parallelizable) →A.4 keeps each child independently mergeable: A.1 ships a working --plan; A.2 ships adapters still consumable by the legacy Go runner; A.3 ships a buildable image + standalone task-executor; A.4 wires it all together. Every parent non-scope item is preserved by explicit non-scope clauses in each child."}
summary: decomposed into fizeau-3e41af01, fizeau-e74ec147, fizeau-ad47f7c8, fizeau-b49d5ea7
